title: The Following
logline: A lonely writer who follows strangers for inspiration becomes entangled with a charismatic burglar and a mysterious blonde woman, only to discover he's been manipulated into becoming the fall guy for murder.
antagonistOrConflict: Cobb (the burglar who orchestrates an elaborate frame-up) and the moral consequences of voyeurism
themes: The consequences of breaking one's own rules
The blurred line between observer and participant

actBreaks.description: The Young Man and Cobb escape to the rooftop after being caught in a burglary, cementing their criminal partnership and the Young Man's transformation.

validation.ruling: Scene #22 ends Act 1 with Cobb challenging the Young Man to pick the next target himself, fundamentally shifting their dynamic and the Young Man's role from passive follower to active participant—an irreversible decision point that propels the story into new, higher-stakes territory.

actBreaks.description: The Young Man breaks into the safe alone, stealing money and fully committing to the crime that will frame him for murder.

validation.ruling: Scene #63 shows the Young Man escaping through a window and fleeing into the night after taping money to his body—a desperate flight that marks the final collapse of his plans and transitions into the interrogation/revelation structure of Act 3, though this is a somewhat diffuse break point.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
Nolan constructs a puzzle-box narrative that fractures chronology into three distinct timelines, identifiable by the Young Man's appearance: long-haired/unshaven (earliest), short-haired/clean (middle), and short-haired/bruised (interrogation frame). The script opens and closes in the interrogation room (scenes 2, 80, 82, 85, 87, 89), creating a confessional frame that the audience initially trusts but ultimately reveals as the Young Man's own deception—he doesn't yet understand he's been set up. The non-linear structure isn't merely stylistic; it's functional, allowing Nolan to withhold the true nature of Cobb and the Blonde's relationship until the final act, while planting clues throughout that reward attentive viewers on repeat viewing. The script moves fluidly between timelines without explicit markers, trusting visual cues and dialogue context to orient the audience.

The three-act structure operates across all timelines simultaneously: Act One establishes the Young Man's voyeuristic hobby and his fateful meeting with Cobb; Act Two deepens his involvement with both Cobb and the Blonde, showing his transformation and growing obsession; Act Three reveals the con, as the interrogation scenes finally catch up to the narrative present and the Young Man realizes he's been framed. The climax occurs not in action but in recognition—the moment the Young Man understands the truth in scene 89, followed by the devastating final image of Cobb walking free in scene 90.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains relentless forward momentum despite its fractured timeline, with an average scene length of 1.4 pages creating a staccato rhythm. Early scenes (1-15) rapidly establish the three timelines through quick cuts and voice-over, disorienting the audience intentionally. The middle section (scenes 16-60) slows slightly as longer dialogue scenes in apartments and restaurants allow character dynamics to develop, particularly the seduction sequences between the Young Man and the Blonde. The final third (scenes 61-90) accelerates dramatically, with scenes 75-89 intercutting between timelines at breakneck speed, creating mounting tension as the various narrative threads converge. Key pacing shifts occur at scene 28 (first extended scene with the Blonde), scene 55 (the restaurant conversation where Cobb's manipulation becomes visible), and the rapid-fire crosscutting of scenes 80-89 that delivers the devastating revelation.

incitingIncident.description: Dark Suit confronts the Young Man at his table, immediately asking who he is and why he's following him, then reveals he's been aware of being followed and invites the Young Man to join him in burglary, explaining his philosophy about taking things to show people what they had.

validation.ruling: Scene #9 shatters the Young Man's status quo when Dark Suit confronts him directly, asking 'Who and why?' This forces the protagonist into the main conflict—he can no longer passively follow people—and establishes the central relationship that drives the story forward.

midpoint.description: At an expensive restaurant, the Young Man confronts The Blonde about choosing a place where they won't encounter the dangerous bald man, and she explains with pity that the Young Man doesn't understand how dangerous he truly is.

validation.ruling: Scene #47 is merely a dinner conversation where the Blonde begins telling a story. Scene #49 (p49, 57%) shows the actual violent event—Baldy smashing a man's fingers with a hammer while forcing the Blonde to watch—which reveals the true danger, raises stakes dramatically, and shifts the Young Man from passive listener to someone who must now act on this knowledge of Baldy's brutality.

climax.description: The Older Man reveals they found the Blonde's body that morning along with a hammer containing two types of blood, and shows the Young Man a shoebox containing stolen items from his flat including the Blonde's underwear, passport photos, and a bloodstained piece of carpet.

validation.ruling: Scene #89 delivers the ultimate revelation and peak dramatic tension: the detective reveals the Blonde is dead, shows evidence planted in the Young Man's flat (including her pearl earring), and the Young Man realizes he's been completely framed for her murder—the final, devastating confrontation that resolves the main story question of who manipulated whom.

scenes.heading: EXT. CROWDED LONDON STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: A Young Man sits in a coffee shop window watching pedestrians pass, explaining in voiceover that loneliness and boredom led him to begin following strangers on the street.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM WITH TABLE - DAY
scenes.summary: In an interrogation-style setting, the bruised and clean-shaven Young Man explains to an Older Man that he followed people to observe them, not for sexual reasons, claiming he's a writer.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUSY STREET IN THE WEST END - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man (long-haired, unshaven) spots a man in a dark suit carrying an overnight bag and begins following him through crowded streets, explaining in voiceover that he created rules to control his compulsion.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROW OF TERRACED TOWNHOUSES - DUSK
scenes.summary: The Young Man (now with short hair and wearing a suit) watches a second-floor window from a bus stop, holding passport photos of a young blonde woman, then a front door opens.

scenes.heading: EXT. SMALL ENTRANCE TO A BASEMENT BAR/CLUB - DUSK
scenes.summary: The Young Man approaches the entrance to a basement bar, glances at the passport photos before pocketing them, looks around nervously, and the scene fades to black.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN SOHO - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man (short-haired, suited, wearing sunglasses with a swollen lip) follows a bald man who enters an office building, then crosses to a café to watch.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFÉ - DAY
scenes.summary: Inside the café, the Young Man removes his sunglasses to examine his badly bruised and swollen eye in a knife's reflection, then notices a woman reading alone.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE A BLOCK OF FLATS IN THE WEST END - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man watches Dark Suit exit a building with a sports bag, follows him to a crowded café, takes a deep breath, and enters.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFÉ - DAY
scenes.summary: Dark Suit confronts the Young Man at his table, immediately asking who he is and why he's following him, then reveals he's been aware of being followed and invites the Young Man to join him in burglary, explaining his philosophy about taking things to show people what they had.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cobb directly confronts the Young Man about following him and recruits him into burglary, initiating the central criminal relationship of the story.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENTRANCE TO A BASEMENT BAR/CLUB - DUSK
scenes.summary: The Blonde enters the basement bar looking suspicious of being followed, and the Young Man (short-haired, clean-shaven) hesitates before following her inside.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT BAR/CLUB - DUSK
scenes.summary: Inside the bar, the Young Man orders a beer and watches The Blonde from along the bar while she remains uninterested in him, and he nervously waits for an opportunity to approach.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENTRANCE TO BASEMENT BAR/CLUB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Outside the club, The Blonde walks past the Young Man without acknowledging him, then they fall into step together and she suggests going to his place because she can't be seen with him locally due to a dangerous bald man.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFÉ - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man sits alone in a café with a notebook, watching the building across the street through the window while wearing sunglasses to hide his bruises.

scenes.heading: EXT. OFFICE BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: From the Young Man's viewpoint in the café, Baldy exits the office building and hails a taxi.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man opens his notebook and writes in it before the scene fades to black.

scenes.heading: INT. OUTSIDE THE DOOR TO A FLAT, THE LANDING OF A NARROW
scenes.summary: Cobb arrives at a flat's door with the Young Man (long-haired, unshaven) behind him, knocks, then puts on rubber surgical gloves and confirms the Young Man is also wearing gloves before they prepare to enter.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb and the Young Man creep through the dark hallway of the flat, checking rooms, until Cobb announces nobody's home and asks the Young Man why he's whispering.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In the bedroom, Cobb finds a bag in the wardrobe and discusses with the Young Man how to determine the occupants' ages and relationship status from their belongings, explaining he burgles for adrenaline and to learn about people, not money.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAT
scenes.summary: In the hallway, the Young Man questions why Cobb planted women's underwear in the male occupant's trousers, and Cobb spins around with manic intensity to explain he wants to show them what they had by taking it away.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb pours wine in the kitchen and offers the Young Man a drink from the occupant's bottle, smoothly opening it with a corkscrew while wearing surgical gloves.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: A woman and older man return home unexpectedly to find Cobb and the Young Man inside; Cobb improvises by claiming they're viewing the flat through an estate agent, confusing the occupants long enough to escape.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP - DAY
scenes.summary: On the rooftop, Cobb and the Young Man wait after their escape, with Cobb laughing about confusing the couple and explaining the older man wasn't the boyfriend, so they were caught during an affair and were glad the intruders weren't the real boyfriend.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the Young Man's living room, The Blonde accidentally breaks a ceramic candlestick while examining it, but the Young Man reveals it was already broken and discards it, then offers her wine while she remains nervous and keeps her coat on.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The bruised Young Man calls Cobb from his window, asking for advice about a job, and Cobb initially refuses but agrees to meet after the Young Man insists he needs help.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE THE DOOR TO A FLAT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb and the Young Man (long-haired, unshaven) put on gloves outside a flat's door, and Cobb finds a key under the doormat before they enter.

scenes.heading: INT. DIMLY LIT HALLWAY OF THE FLAT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb proceeds down the dimly lit hallway checking rooms while instructing the Young Man to find a bag.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In the living room, Cobb circles the space examining items and declares there's nothing worth taking, then deliberately knocks a ceramic candlestick to the floor where it breaks, dismissing the Young Man's suggestions about the stereo and CDs as worthless.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man chases Cobb to the front door asking if they'll take anything, but Cobb refuses to steal from poor people and mentions another place he's already checked out before they leave.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROW OF TERRACED TOWNHOUSES - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man (short-haired, clean-shaven) watches from a bus stop as The Blonde appears at her window, then Baldy exits the building and walks away, prompting the Young Man to ring The Blonde's buzzer and enter when the door unlocks.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE THE BLONDE’S FLAT - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man knocks on The Blonde's door and it pushes open slightly, and she calls from inside that it's open.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL OF A LARGE APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man enters the flat where a shower is running, The Blonde emerges in a bathrobe saying he's early, then disappears into the bathroom telling him to make himself at home while he peers at the half-closed bathroom door.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In the expensively decorated living room, the Young Man examines a bare table surface, sits in an armchair, and The Blonde enters in her robe to discuss the burglary, revealing someone stole her CD player, CDs, and used one of her bags to carry the items.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man pauses at a door that's open a crack, looking through into the bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Through the crack, the Young Man watches The Blonde buttoning her blouse in front of a mirror, then pushes the door open, approaches her, pulls her toward him, and kisses her before the scene fades to black.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Young Man and The Blonde become physically intimate, deepening his involvement with her and complicating his relationship with both her and Cobb.

scenes.heading: EXT. OFFICE BUILDING IN SOHO - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man (short-haired, wearing dark glasses) watches Baldy leave with another man in a cab, then walks down an alley studying a floor plan and counting windows to identify a specific one.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In his bedroom, the bruised Young Man puts on rubber surgical gloves, removes a new hammer from a paper bag, and tests its weight in his gloved hands.

scenes.heading: INT. INSIDE THE HALL OF A LARGE APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: The front door of the large apartment splinters inward as Cobb and the Young Man (long-haired, unshaven) break in wearing gloves, then cautiously enter and begin opening doors to let light into the hall.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cobb and the Young Man break into The Blonde's apartment, marking the convergence of the Young Man's criminal activity with his romantic involvement.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: From the Young Man's viewpoint, the living room appears expensively decorated with many pictures and plants.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: In the hall, the Young Man whispers approval of the place to Cobb, who speaks normally and instructs him to check out the stuff while Cobb searches bedrooms for a bag.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man enters the living room and scans it, then notices and examines a collection of framed photographs on the wall showing the same attractive blonde woman in various poses, including professional modeling shots.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Young Man discovers he has broken into The Blonde's apartment, realizing the connection between his burglary activities with Cobb and his romantic involvement.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man exits the living room and walks down the corridor looking for Cobb, entering a room near the front door.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In the feminine bedroom, Cobb holds up a garter belt from an open drawer and makes suggestive comments while the Young Man recognizes The Blonde's photograph above the chest of drawers, and Cobb rummages through silk panties while discussing her appearance.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man stuffs CDs into a leather holdall while Cobb watches from an armchair, then the Young Man adds the CD player and discovers passport photographs of The Blonde on a desk, questioning why she has so many pictures of herself.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROW OF TERRACED TOWNHOUSES - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb and the Young Man exit the front door with the Young Man carrying the full leather holdall, then walk briskly away down the road.

scenes.heading: INT. CHARING CROSS STATION LEFT-LUGGAGE OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man deposits the leather holdall at the left-luggage office and receives a ticket, which Cobb refuses to take, telling him to keep the stolen items since it was his job.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHARING CROSS STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Outside the station, Cobb tells the Young Man to pick up the items tomorrow and wait to fence them, then suggests the Young Man could sell them himself, and Cobb apologizes for being hard on him earlier, explaining he won't let anyone put him at risk.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At an expensive restaurant, the Young Man confronts The Blonde about choosing a place where they won't encounter the dangerous bald man, and she explains with pity that the Young Man doesn't understand how dangerous he truly is.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S FLAT, FRONT HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: A heavy-set man opens The Blonde's door to admit a smaller man while The Blonde explains in voiceover that this man had cheated them out of money.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S FLAT, LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In The Blonde's living room, two heavy-set men pin the smaller man to the floor while Baldy smashes his finger with a hammer, then orders The Blonde to stay and watch as he continues smashing the man's fingers.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Baldy brings a hammer down sharply, stands up and drops it with blood visible, while the Blonde cries and Baldy demands a tea-towel.
scenes.turningPointNote: A violent act occurs in the Blonde's living room involving Baldy and a hammer, establishing the dangerous reality of her situation

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Blonde confirms the hammer story is true, then becomes furious when the Young Man asks if she worked for Baldy, calling him sick and a hypocrite before throwing down her napkin and leaving.

scenes.heading: EXT. OFFICE BUILDING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man with short hair and sunglasses enters a side alleyway, counts windows along an office building, and uses a hammer's claw to lever open a window before climbing through.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man climbs through the office window, removes books from shelves to uncover a wall safe, then retrieves a piece of paper from his pocket with a series of numbers on it.

scenes.heading: INT. FRENCH RESTAURANT - DUSK
scenes.summary: Cobb tells the Young Man he's developing a taste for violating and voyeurism, then throws a credit card on the table saying the Young Man will pay for the expensive meal using Timothy Kerr's card.

scenes.heading: INT. FRENCH RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man notices a young woman approaching their table and panics, telling Cobb she's coming their way, and Cobb nods toward a door.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRENCH RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cobb exits the restaurant and turns on the Young Man angrily, complaining that he hates not finishing a good meal with coffee, then waves at a taxi.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OF TAXI - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the taxi, the Young Man explains he's nervous about being recognized, and Cobb suggests changing his appearance with a haircut and smart clothes so his own mother won't recognize him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROW OF TERRACED TOWNHOUSES - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man with short hair and clean-shaven face buzzes the Blonde's flat, she tells him to fuck off, but he asks to apologize and she clicks the lock open.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S FLAT, HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man walks down the corridor and sees the Blonde sitting on the bed, then stands in the bedroom doorway.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man confesses he's been researching burglaries by watching someone break into houses, then accuses the Blonde of still seeing the bald guy, which she denies before he reveals he followed her and saw them together.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man with short hair and bruised face frantically tapes bundles of money to his arms, waist, and bare legs, working in a frenzy to cover his body with cash.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man falls out of a window onto the ground, struggles to his feet weaving like a drunk, and heads toward the main street.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man examines his longish hair and beard in the bathroom mirror, then picks up nail scissors and starts cutting his hair.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man knots a tie wearing a dark suit with dramatically shorter hair, examines his reflection, then picks up passport photos of the Blonde from the mirror frame and looks at them thoughtfully.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man calls someone on the phone and waits for them to answer, hearing a male voice respond.

scenes.heading: INT. INDISTINCT SURROUNDINGS; A WINDOW COVERED BY A NET
scenes.summary: Cobb in a white unbuttoned shirt stands by a window on the phone asking what he can do for the caller.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man tells Cobb he's met a buyer and will fence the stolen items himself for half the proceeds, while holding passport photos and silk, then mentions he cut his hair and got new clothes.

scenes.heading: INT. BY THE WINDOW - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb hangs up the phone in a bedroom, revealing the Blonde is lying naked in bed, and tells her the Young Man is going to hang on to her stuff and probably try to sell it back to her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals Cobb and the Blonde are working together and have set up the Young Man as part of a scheme

scenes.heading: INT. YOUNG MAN’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man knots his tie while the Blonde lies on his bed, and she asks what if someone won't help, to which he says he'll do it on his own.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHARING CROSS STATION LEFT-LUGGAGE OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man hands over a ticket at the left-luggage office and collects a leather holdall.

scenes.heading: EXT. HUNGERFORD BRIDGE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man crosses Hungerford Bridge heading south while carrying the leather holdall.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY IN A BLOCK OF FLATS - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man knocks on Cobb's door, and Cobb opens it saying he's late and questioning why he brought the holdall instead of fencing it.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man asks Cobb to help break into a club-owner's office safe for photos and probably money, and when Cobb demands to know what's going on, the Young Man reveals a bald guy has photos of a friend and is blackmailing her.

scenes.heading: INT. COBB’S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cobb asks the Blonde if she had to sleep with the Young Man, she says he told her to, he clarifies that's not the same, then admits he beat the Young Man because once told about their relationship he had to react realistically since he's in deep trouble with people who think he was involved in something.

scenes.heading: EXT. NARROW PASSAGEWAY CONNECTING TWO STREETS LIT BY A
scenes.summary: The Young Man with short hair and shades stumbles along a rainy passageway in a bulging dark suit with a fifty-pound note sticking out of his cuff, then disappears around the corner.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man removes money taped to his wet, bloodied body while on the phone, telling the Blonde he got the items and had to stop to dump a lot of money, giggling and yelping as he yanks tape from his legs.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE THE BLONDE’S FLAT
scenes.summary: The Young Man looks up at the Blonde's window and is not happy.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S FRONT HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Young Man slaps the Blonde hard upon entering, accuses her of lying to get money, then grabs and shakes her demanding explanation before she reveals the photos were for a friend who needs a decoy suspect, and when he asks who the friend is, she says it's Cobb.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Young Man discovers he's been set up as a patsy by both Cobb and the Blonde

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM WITH TABLE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Young Man with short hair and bruises finishes telling his story to an Older Man at a table with a tape recorder, and the Older Man says he has one or two questions.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN THE WEST END - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb strolls down a West End street carrying the leather holdall.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM WITH TABLE AND TAPE RECORDER - DAY
scenes.summary: The Older Man tells the Young Man there are no unsolved murders of old ladies and they don't know anyone named Cobb, leaving the Young Man confused and scared.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Young Man learns the entire murder investigation was fabricated and Cobb doesn't exist in police records

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S FRONT HALL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Blonde opens her front door to Cobb.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb tells the Blonde he warned her the Young Man would look in the envelope, calls him a born peeper, then takes out a handkerchief and starts wiping the outside of his glass.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM WITH TABLE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Older Man asks the Young Man if there's something else he'd like to tell him about while the Young Man thinks hard, puzzled.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb puts on rubber gloves, retrieves a hammer from a shopping bag with dried blood on its head, and tells the Blonde the old man was specific about how he should do things.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM WITH TABLE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Older Man puts on rubber surgical gloves and asks the Young Man if there's anything at all he'd like to say about his side of things.

scenes.heading: INT. THE BLONDE’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb approaches the Blonde with the hammer, telling her an old man says her blackmail demands became too greedy and he was specific about where and how to take care of things, mentioning a bloodstained carpet she has stashed away, while the crying Blonde begs him not to.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM WITH TABLE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Older Man reveals they found the Blonde's body that morning along with a hammer containing two types of blood, and shows the Young Man a shoebox containing stolen items from his flat including the Blonde's underwear, passport photos, and a bloodstained piece of carpet.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Young Man learns the Blonde has been murdered and he's been completely framed with physical evidence planted in his flat

scenes.heading: EXT. BUSY WEST END STREET CROWDED WITH PEDESTRIANS - DAY
scenes.summary: Cobb exits a café, walks down a busy West End street, stops to look back over his shoulder to check if anyone is following him, then turns back and disappears into the crowd of pedestrians.
